threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the Overground' for the Dalston Junction line. Classic newbie mistake.
Caught a night bus home from Brixton, and the driver had a full-on rant about how no one appreciates the late-night shift. You could feel the tiredness in the air.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an artist painting a mural while a group of kids played soccer on the same street—classic Neukölln vibes.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how some U-Bahn stations have their own unique art installations? Like the surreal tiles at Kottbusser Tor—each telling a different story.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you can spot a Muni delay a mile away—which is basically every day now, but especially at Civic Center. Makes you nostalgic for the old days.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 49 stuck at Civic Center today, dog on the tracks. The conductor couldn’t help but mutter, 'Only in SF.'

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all of Gràcia is gaudy Gaudí. They miss the local bakeries where the croissants have a secret spice mix that nobody talks about.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still looking at a street map instead of just following Google Maps. You can spot them fumbling in front of the Passeig de Gràcia.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a flat white and look puzzled when it takes longer than five minutes. Give it time, mate, that's just how it goes.
Rode the 59 past Coldharbour Lane; saw a couple arguing over who gets the last slice of pizza. Bet they didn’t notice the bus driver smirking.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the U-Bahn stopped at Hermannplatz and everyone just kept talking, like the train's presence was as normal as a dog barking.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a döner shop open at 4 AM, serving fresh meat to those stumbling home from a techno club. It's a rite of passage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you don’t flinch at the BART delays but lose it over a dog on the Muni tracks—priorities, right?

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: You know it’s bad when the dog on the tracks is the highlight of the delays. Muni's got us all playing the waiting game again.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best jamón is at La Boqueria – but locals know the real gems are the tiny shops in Gràcia that sell it by the slice.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they order their vermut—too much ice, no idea about the olive garnish. It's a small detail, but it sticks out.
